meta.notes: Queries authored by spike operator. Candidates retrieved via SQLite FTS5 BM25 (title+content) from parent data/news.db. Items with insufficient BM25 hits padded with random items from the corpus; such padded items will mostly be labeled 0, which is desirable (adds negatives). Labeler: single-call-per-batch, 10 items/call, temperature=0. Parse failures on an API error are retried once, then default to label=0 for the whole batch.

candidates.title: Preventing overfitting in deep learning using differential privacy
candidates.summary: This paper proposes using differential privacy to prevent overfitting in deep learning. The approach has implications for improving the robustness of deep neural networks. The method is based on adding noise to the training data to prevent the model from memorizing individual data points.

candidates.title: Computational Hermeneutics: Evaluating generative AI as a cultural technology
candidates.summary: The paper investigates Generative AI as a cultural technology, arguing for new evaluation frameworks rooted in hermeneutics. It suggests AI should be interpreted through contextual and cultural lenses rather than just metrics.

candidates.title: Mapping High-Performance Regions in Battery Scheduling across Data Uncertainty, Battery Design, and Planning Horizons
candidates.summary: This research analyzes battery scheduling performance by mapping the interplay between uncertainty, design parameters, and planning horizons. It uses predictive control models to optimize energy storage management under varying data conditions.

candidates.title: Weak-to-Strong Knowledge Distillation Accelerates Visual Learning
candidates.summary: The study investigates weak-to-strong knowledge distillation to accelerate the training of robust student models. By utilizing a frozen teacher during early training phases, it significantly reduces the overall computational cost of visual learning.

candidates.title: A Discordance-Aware Multimodal Framework with Multi-Agent Clinical Reasoning
candidates.summary: This paper proposes a discordance-aware multimodal framework that combines machine learning prediction models with a tool grounded multi-agent reasoning system. The approach has implications for improving the clinical interpretation and patient stratification of knee osteoarthritis. The framework is designed to model the mismatch between structural damage and patient-reported symptoms.

candidates.title: mrdbourke/pytorch-deep-learning

candidates.title: Annotation Entropy Predicts Per-Example Learning Dynamics in LoRA Fine-Tuning
candidates.summary: This paper finds that LoRA fine-tuning exhibits un-learning on contested examples, which show increasing loss during training. The discovery emerges from correlating annotation entropy with per-example area under the loss curve. The approach has implications for improving the understanding of LoRA fine-tuning.

candidates.title: Open Source Self-Driving with Comma AI
